result:
## Summary

The site had pivoted from **InboxZap** (email cleanup tool) to **Lumina** (AI meeting assistant), but the SEO layer never caught up — which explains the GSC data: strong brand rankings for "inboxzap" but the 12 blog posts driving thousands of monthly impressions were stuck at position 40-60 with near-zero clicks.

**Bugs found and fixed:**
1. **Metadata/content mismatch** — `layout.tsx` still had InboxZap title/description/OG tags while the homepage content was already Lumina. Fixed, added Organization + SoftwareApplication JSON-LD.
2. **Blog was completely broken** — the index page was client-rendered and fetched `/api/posts`, a route that doesn't exist anywhere in the repo. It just showed a permanent loading spinner. Rebuilt as a server component.
3. **No individual blog post routes existed in the repo at all** — despite 12 posts generating real GSC impressions. Added `app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx` (SSR, `generateMetadata`, Article JSON-LD) backed by a new `lib/posts.ts` content module.
4. **Zero meta descriptions on any blog post** — added real ones to all 12.
5. **`/features` and `/pricing` didn't exist in the repo** despite being linked from nav/hero — added both (also fixed a duplicate-`<h1>` bug on Features).
6. **`/faq` 301-redirected to `/#faq`, which didn't exist** — dead end. Added a real FAQ section with FAQPage JSON-LD.
7. **Sitemap had a TODO leaving out every blog post** — fixed; all 20 routes now build and are listed.

**Content strategy fix:** rather than discard the 12 legacy posts (real indexation/backlink equity) or leave them off-topic, each now ends with a contextual bridge tying its email/inbox topic back to a genuine Lumina capability, plus a link to `/features` or `/pricing`. The FAQ also includes a direct "What happened to InboxZap?" entry to protect the still-valuable branded search traffic (14 clicks/mo at position 4.1) instead of confusing those visitors with an unrelated product.

Verified with `next build` (all 20 routes compile and prerender) and a local production server — checked status codes, title/meta tags, sitemap XML, and the FAQ redirect end-to-end.

**Not done, worth knowing:** `/img/og-lumina.png` referenced in OG tags doesn't exist anywhere (`public/` only has `robots.txt`) — social share previews are currently broken; that needs a real image asset, which I can't fabricate. Longer-term, the 12 legacy posts are still fundamentally about email management, not meetings — the bridge CTAs are a stopgap, not a substitute for a net-new content plan targeting actual Lumina keywords (meeting notes, transcription, action items).
